meta.title: Mercedes S-Class W223 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

itemListElement.name: M256 E30 DEH LA G Engine
M256 E30 DEH LA GR Engine (PHEV)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes W223 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned W223 engine typically costs £4,000–£20,000 supply only, depending on engine — the volume S 350 d diesel sits at £4,000–£6,500. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes W223 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM656 diesel in the S 350 d and S 400 d is the most proven W223 engine — a refined straight-six with strong economy and a reputation for durability from its W222 predecessor. The M256 petrol straight-six (S 500) is also a solid choice, though its 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity. All W223 engines are relatively new; long-term reliability data is still emerging.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes W223?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 48V mild-hybrid system can experience battery degradation or ISG motor failure from 60,000 miles. The OM656 diesel's AdBlue system can suffer from crystallisation, NOx sensor failure or pump issues. The M176/M177 V8s suffer from turbo coolant line degradation (hot-vee design). The E-ACTIVE BODY CONTROL air suspension can develop leaks or compressor failure. The W223 is a highly complex vehicle; specialist maintenance is essential.

The W223 is a new generation, and long-term reliability data is still emerging. Early indications suggest the powertrains are robust, with the OM656 diesel and M256 petrol proving reliable in other Mercedes models. The complexity of the 48V hybrid and E-ACTIVE BODY CONTROL systems presents new potential failure points. Regular maintenance at a Mercedes specialist is essential to avoid costly repairs.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes W223 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AMG S 63 E Performance is the most complex and expensive engine in the range — while it's a technological tour-de-force, the combination of V8, PHEV and complex electronics means it requires specialist maintenance and has higher running costs. The V12 S 680 is also a significant commitment — parts are rare, specialist knowledge is essential, and running costs are very high.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my W223 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue warnings and countdowns (OM656 diesel), "Check Engine" lights related to the 48V system, coolant loss or overheating (V8s), or unusual noises from the suspension are key warnings. Sudden loss of power, rough running or warning messages on the MBUX screen also warrant immediate inspection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes W223 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: The W223's high residual values — a well-kept example can be worth £40,000–£80,000 — mean engine replacement is almost always financially viable. A £4,000–£6,500 recon on an S 350 d sits at 5–16% of the car's value. Even the V12's £12,000–£20,000 recon sits at 25–50% of a top-end example's value. Replacement is generally justifiable if the car is otherwise in excellent condition.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes W223 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM656 diesel and M256 petrol are expected to exceed 150,000–200,000 miles with proper maintenance, based on their W222 predecessors. The 48V battery is a consumable item, expected to last 80,000–120,000 miles. The V8 and V12 engines can also reach high mileages but require more frequent maintenance and have higher running costs.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the W223, V223 and Z223?

acceptedAnswer.text: The W223 is the standard-wheelbase S-Class saloon. The V223 is the long-wheelbase version offering additional rear legroom. The Z223 is the Mercedes-Maybach S-Class, featuring extended wheelbase, unique styling and even higher levels of luxury. All share the same engine range, though the Maybach range is limited to the S 580 e, S 680 and, in some markets, the S 580. Engine costs are identical; body-specific parts and maintenance differ.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes W223 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen, lower passenger side), stamped on the engine block, and detailed in your vehicle registration documents (V5C). Tell us your variant (S 350 d, AMG S 63 E Performance, etc.) and approximate registration date, and we'll confirm it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will verify it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes S-Class W223 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the seventh-generation S-Class — from the straight-six petrols and diesels to the biturbo V8, the V12 and the electrified AMG flagship. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes S-Class W223 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the W223/V223 chassis — from the M256 straight-six petrols to the OM656 diesels, the M176/M177 V8s and the M279 V12.

overview.intro: The Mercedes-Benz S-Class W223 — the seventh generation of the flagship saloon — was unveiled in September 2020 as the most technologically advanced S-Class yet. Built on the MRA2 platform, the W223 introduced second-generation MBUX with a 12.3-inch digital instrument cluster and a 12.8-inch OLED portrait touchscreen. The engine range carries over from the W222 with inline-six, V8 and V12 options, all featuring 48V mild-hybrid EQ Boost technology. For the first time, the V12 engine is fitted with 4MATIC all-wheel drive, exclusive to the S 680 Guard 4MATIC. Plug-in hybrid variants (S 580 e) and the AMG S 63 E Performance joined the range from 2022 onwards. The W223 is offered in both standard (W223) and long-wheelbase (V223) forms.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes MRA2 • Predecessor: W222 (2013–2020) • Successor: Currently in production • Sibling body styles in this generation: V223 (long wheelbase), Z223 (Mercedes-Maybach).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No [MB-VERIFIED] enquiry data exists for the S-Class W223 — the model is absent from EM_Proprietary_Data's per-model table, reflecting its low-volume UK status as the brand's flagship.

items.quote: The W223 S-Class engine range represents the pinnacle of Mercedes powertrain technology, with electrification now standard across the board. For most buyers, the S 350 d (OM656 diesel) is the sensible choice: a refined, economical straight-six that delivers 286 bhp and 600 Nm of torque — more than adequate for wafting duties. The S 500 (M256 petrol) offers 435 bhp from a smooth straight-six with 48V mild-hybrid assistance — a genuinely effortless drivetrain. The S 580 (M176 V8) delivers 503 bhp from the 4.0-litre biturbo V8, combining effortless power with surprising efficiency thanks to cylinder deactivation and mild-hybrid tech. The AMG S 63 E Performance is a technological tour-de-force: 603 bhp from the V8 plus 188 bhp from the electric motor, producing a combined 750 Nm of torque — but it's complex and expensive. The S 680 (M279 V12) is the range-topper: 612 bhp and 830 Nm of torque from a 6.0-litre biturbo V12, but it's reserved for the armoured S 680 Guard and Mercedes-Maybach variants. Buying instruction: for most buyers, the S 350 d offers the best balance of refinement, economy and reliability; the S 500 petrol is the pick for those who want the smoothest straight-six experience. The V8 and V12 variants are for those who value the badge and the experience over running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Smooth and refined — early days for reliability data
Range-topper — for the few who can afford it
More power — same reliability as S 350 d
Latest diesel — 367 bhp with mild hybrid

economicsRule.title: The W223 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,000–£6,500 S 350 d recon against a £40,000–£80,000 W223 sits at 5–16% of the car's value — well within the 70% Mercedes threshold for almost every example. Even the most expensive recon (£20,000 for the V12) sits at 25–50% of a well-kept example's value. The W223's high residual values mean engine replacement is almost always financially viable — the question is whether the car's overall condition justifies the spend.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W223 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: S 350 d (OM656) — refined diesel, lower running costs, strong economy.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W223 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: AMG S 63 E Performance (M177) — PHEV complexity, AMG-specific parts, high running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W223 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: S 350 d (OM656) — the most sensible engine in the range with strong economy and refinement.

problems.title: 48V Mild-Hybrid System Failures
problems.affectedModels: All W223 variants with EQ Boost (M256 petrol, M176 V8, OM656 diesel).
problems.rootCause: The 48V electrical system that powers the integrated starter-generator (ISG) and supports the mild-hybrid function can experience battery degradation, voltage regulator issues or ISG motor failure. Symptoms include the "Check Engine" light, reduced power, or the system failing to restart the engine after coasting.
problems.recommendation: On any W223 approaching 60,000 miles, have the 48V system inspected — a £300–£700 preventative check is cheaper than the £1,200–£2,500 replacement that follows if ignored. The 48V battery is a consumable item; budget for replacement at 80,000–100,000 miles.

problems.title: OM656 AdBlue System Issues
problems.affectedModels: W223 S 350 d, S 400 d, S 450 d (OM656 diesel).
problems.rootCause: The AdBlue (DEF) system on the OM656 diesel engine can suffer from crystallisation in the injector, NOx sensor failure, or pump issues. This triggers the "AdBlue system malfunction" warning, which will eventually prevent the car from restarting after a countdown.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of an AdBlue warning, get the system inspected immediately — a £250–£500 preventative cleaning is far cheaper than the £900–£1,800 replacement that follows if ignored. Regular AdBlue top-ups using the correct fluid are essential.

cta.label: Compare OM656 AdBlue repair quotes for your W223 →

problems.title: M176/M177 V8 Turbo Coolant Line Degradation
problems.affectedModels: W223 S 580 (M176), AMG S 63 E Performance (M177).
problems.rootCause: The twin-turbo V8's "hot-vee" design places the turbos inside the V of the engine, generating significant heat. This causes the turbo coolant lines to degrade prematurely, leading to coolant leaks. The M177 in the AMG S 63 E also adds the complexity of the PHEV system.
problems.recommendation: On any V8-engined W223, budget for a coolant system inspection at 60,000 miles — a £1,000–£1,800 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 rebuild that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare M176/M177 engine prices for your W223 →

problems.title: Air Suspension (Airmatic) Failures
problems.affectedModels: All W223 variants with Airmatic air suspension (standard on all UK models).
problems.rootCause: The Airmatic air suspension system uses air struts and a compressor that can fail over time. The struts develop leaks, the compressor overheats and fails, or the valve block malfunctions. The W223's E-ACTIVE BODY CONTROL system adds further complexity.
problems.recommendation: On any W223 over 60,000 miles, have the Airmatic system inspected — a £300–£600 preventative repair is cheaper than the £1,500–£3,000 replacement that follows if ignored. The compressor is a common failure point on higher-mileage examples.

cta.label: Compare W223 air suspension repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: W223 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are W223/V223 (S-Class, 2020–present) only. The S 450 4MATIC (M256, 367 bhp) is available in select markets but omitted from the UK core listing due to limited availability; UK-market S-Class models typically start with the S 500. The S 680 Guard 4MATIC is the armoured variant; the Mercedes-Maybach S-Class (Z223) shares the same engine codes but is covered on a separate Maybach Generation Page where body-specific parts and costs differ. The AMG S 63 E Performance was introduced in 2023.
